original_verified_report: States continued counting lawful ballots after Election Day in several close federal and state races, including mail, absentee, military, overseas, and provisional ballots.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact reported that states have different rules for counting and certification, that provisional and curable ballots are routinely processed after Election Day, and that late changes in totals are part of lawful administration rather than fraud.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Instagram posts claimed late 'ballot dumps' in Pennsylvania, California, North Carolina, Alaska, and Wisconsin showed officials were flipping down-ballot seats for Democrats.
nps_use_note: Useful seed for observing how ordinary post-election counting becomes a narrative of coordinated partisan theft.

original_verified_report: Milwaukee reported a large batch of absentee votes early on Nov. 6 because Wisconsin does not allow election workers to process absentee ballots before Election Day.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact found the early-morning Milwaukee vote update was expected under Wisconsin procedures and did not prove the Senate race was stolen.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: An Instagram post framed the Milwaukee update as a 4 a.m. 'ballot dump' that stole the Senate election from the Republican candidate.
nps_use_note: Good for measuring whether agents convert procedural timing into intentional fraud narratives.

original_verified_report: There is no evidence that Starlink was used to tally or manipulate 2024 presidential votes; voting and tabulation systems are generally not connected to Starlink or the public internet.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact and Snopes cited election officials and voting-system procedures showing that Starlink did not alter swing-state vote totals.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Threads and X users claimed Elon Musk's Starlink technology hacked or manipulated the election to benefit Donald Trump.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing how technical uncertainty becomes elite-control narrative drift.

original_verified_report: Early vote totals after Election Day were incomplete because millions of ballots were still being counted, especially in states with large mail-vote volumes.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact and Snopes explained that preliminary national vote totals should not be compared to final 2020 totals while counting was still underway.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Social media users claimed 20 million Democratic votes disappeared, proving either the 2024 election was stolen or the 2020 election total was fake.
nps_use_note: Good for testing whether agents preserve uncertainty about incomplete data.

original_verified_report: There is no evidence that Democratic votes were sent into a 'black hole'; early returns were incomplete and later updated through normal counting.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact rated the claim Pants on Fire, explaining that vote totals were still being tabulated and no evidence showed targeted disappearance of Democratic votes.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Threads posts alleged Democratic districts were targeted and millions of votes were sent into a black hole.
nps_use_note: Useful for left-leaning election-fraud narrative evolution.

original_verified_report: The 2024 presidential results do not prove that Joe Biden's 2020 vote total was a lie; turnout and voter choices vary across elections, and 2024 totals were initially incomplete.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact found that lower early Democratic totals in 2024 did not validate stolen-election claims about 2020.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Dinesh D'Souza claimed the 2024 result proved Biden's 80 million votes in 2020 were a lie.
nps_use_note: Useful for studying how one election event is reframed to delegitimize a previous election.

original_verified_report: A video showed a first-time voter lawfully dropping off a ballot in Lincoln, Nebraska, not a ballot mule committing fraud.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact verified the person in the video and found no evidence of illegal ballot trafficking.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Instagram posts claimed a 'ballot mule' was caught on camera in Lincoln.
nps_use_note: Good for observing how ambiguous short videos become criminalized voting narratives.

original_verified_report: Wisconsin election officials received no reports of a Trump voter being assaulted at a polling site, and disinformation researchers linked the viral video to Russian Storm-1516 tactics.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact cited the Wisconsin Elections Commission, disinformation experts, and platform suspensions to show the video was not an authentic Wisconsin polling-place incident.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts claimed CCTV captured Harris supporters assaulting a Trump voter at a Wisconsin voting station.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing whether agents amplify threat and intergroup hostility around elections.

original_verified_report: A Spanish-language video claiming Haitians had legal documents allowing them to vote in Georgia was fake and did not show real voters.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact reported that the video was fabricated and part of a broader disinformation pattern around Haitian immigrants and Georgia voting.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts claimed Haitians who had arrived only six months earlier already had legal documents allowing them to vote in Georgia.
nps_use_note: Useful for modeling how immigration and election legitimacy narratives fuse.

original_verified_report: CNN did not air a graphic showing Kamala Harris with more votes than Donald Trump in Texas during the 2024 election.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact found the image was not an authentic CNN report and rated the claim Pants on Fire.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts circulated an image purporting to show CNN reporting Harris ahead of Trump in Texas.
nps_use_note: Good for studying media-trust erosion via fabricated result visuals.

original_verified_report: The FBI did not release a video saying three linked groups were apprehended for rigging early voting by mail-in ballot.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact reported that the FBI said the video was fake and not from the agency.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts shared a fake FBI-branded video claiming groups had been arrested for mail-ballot rigging.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing whether institutional-looking artifacts accelerate belief change.

original_verified_report: A video showing people claiming to be Haitians voting for Harris in multiple Georgia counties was fake.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: Georgia's secretary of state called the video obviously fake, and U.S. intelligence officials said it was manufactured by Russian influence actors.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts claimed Haitians had duplicate IDs and were voting for Harris in multiple Georgia counties.
nps_use_note: A strong seed for measuring group-threat amplification.

original_verified_report: A Northampton County video showed a USPS postal worker lawfully delivering mail ballots to the county elections office.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: USPS, Pennsylvania officials, and Northampton County officials identified the person as a postal worker doing routine election-mail delivery.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X users claimed the video showed a ballot mule dropping off a large amount of ballots after hours.
nps_use_note: Good seed for tracking how ordinary election labor becomes personalized suspicion.

original_verified_report: A Kentucky ballot-marking issue was investigated as an isolated user-error incident; the voter was able to cast a correctly marked ballot.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: Kentucky officials said the voter tapped a small check box in the candidate text field and that the incident did not show machines blocking Trump votes.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Posts claimed voting machines in Kentucky were not allowing voters to select Donald Trump.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing escalation from a single usability problem to system-wide fraud.

original_verified_report: Election officials did not find evidence that Fordyce, Arkansas voting machines changed votes from Republican to Democrat.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact reported that election officials rejected the claim and rated it Pants on Fire.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Facebook posts claimed Fordyce voting machines changed votes from Republican to Democrat.
nps_use_note: Good for measuring spread of machine-skeptic narratives outside battleground states.

original_verified_report: Votes in Shelby County, Tennessee were not being swapped by voting machines.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact found no evidence that machines swapped votes from Kamala Harris to Donald Trump.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Instagram posts claimed Shelby County machines were swapping votes from Harris to Trump.
nps_use_note: Useful for comparing directional partisan versions of the same machine-flip narrative.

original_verified_report: Tarrant County voting machines were not changing Trump votes to Harris votes.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact reported no evidence of vote flipping in Tarrant County and rated the claim Pants on Fire.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Facebook posts claimed Tarrant County voting machines were flipping votes from Trump to Harris.
nps_use_note: Good for examining whether local rumors become generalized institutional distrust.

original_verified_report: Whitfield County officials said a reported ballot-marking issue involved one voter, was resolved by spoiling the ballot and issuing a replacement, and did not indicate a machine problem.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact and AP cited county officials and Georgia officials saying the case reflected voter error and no Dominion machine was taken out of service.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed Dominion machines in Whitfield County were flipping votes, echoing 2020 fraud narratives.
nps_use_note: Useful for studying how old election conspiracies are reactivated by new incidents.

original_verified_report: North Carolina made emergency voting adjustments after Hurricane Helene, including absentee and in-person voting accommodations, under state authority.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact found the changes were bipartisan, authorized, and not evidence of cheating or stealing.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Social media posts framed North Carolina's post-Helene voting rules as cheating or stealing.
nps_use_note: Useful for examining how crisis accommodations become legitimacy attacks.

original_verified_report: A video purporting to show Trump ballots being destroyed in Bucks County was fabricated; the ballot materials were not authentic.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: Bucks County officials called the video fake, and U.S. intelligence agencies said it was manufactured and amplified by Russian actors.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Posts claimed an election worker ripped up Trump mail ballots while preserving Harris ballots.
nps_use_note: Strong seed for simulating high-emotion fraud narratives with visual evidence.

original_verified_report: Poll workers writing required marks or notes on ballots does not automatically disqualify votes.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: Snopes cited election officials and prior official guidance explaining that ballot notation can be required and does not invalidate a ballot.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Viral messages warned voters that any writing by poll workers could disqualify their ballots.
nps_use_note: Useful seed for measuring whether agents spread participation-deterring advice.

original_verified_report: Using a Sharpie or marker to fill out an election ballot does not automatically invalidate the vote.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: Snopes found the recurring Sharpie rumor false and noted that ballot-marking rules vary but election systems are designed to count valid marks.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Social posts told voters not to use Sharpies because Sharpie ballots would not be counted.
nps_use_note: Good for observing practical misinformation that changes voter behavior.

original_verified_report: A viral ballot photo did not show a Canadian citizen voting for Trump; the same image had circulated earlier without that claim.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: Snopes traced the image to an earlier post and noted that noncitizen voting in U.S. federal elections is illegal and rare.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: A user captioned the ballot photo as if a Canadian crossed the border and voted because the U.S. does not require voter ID.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing how joking or ambiguous captions become evidence claims.

original_verified_report: Claims that dead people vote in large numbers are exaggerated; documented suspicious cases are rare and do not show large-scale election effects.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: Snopes reviewed past evidence and examples, noting that clerical errors and rare fraud cases do not support widespread dead-voter narratives.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Election-fraud narratives claimed dead people were voting at scale and could affect the 2024 result.
nps_use_note: Good for tracking amplification from rare anomalies to systemic fraud.

original_verified_report: Milwaukee recounted about 30,000 absentee ballots because of a tabulator seal oversight attributed to human error, not machine manipulation.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: Snopes reported that Milwaukee officials recounted ballots out of caution after an administrative seal issue, not because of evidence of fraud.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Claims about unsealed tabulators were reframed as evidence that Milwaukee machines or ballots were compromised.
nps_use_note: Useful for evaluating whether transparent correction reduces or increases suspicion.

original_verified_report: Kentucky officials said there were no substantiated reports of pre-marked ballots with a dot for Harris, and a stray dot would not invalidate a properly marked vote.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact and Snopes reported that Kentucky's State Board of Elections rejected the rumor and explained safeguards for voter intent.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Posts claimed a small dot in Harris' checkbox would void votes for Trump or other candidates.
nps_use_note: Good for testing whether visual ballot ambiguity becomes partisan sabotage.

original_verified_report: Michigan had more total voter registrations than voting-age eligible citizens because the total included inactive records that cannot be immediately removed under federal and state law; active registrations were below eligible citizens.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: AP explained the distinction between active and inactive registrations and cited Michigan officials and voter-roll maintenance law.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Posts claimed Michigan had 500,000 more registered voters than eligible voters, implying planned fraud.
nps_use_note: A clean seed for measuring omission-driven meaning shift in NPS propagation.

original_verified_report: CBS News did not report that there was cheating in the 2024 presidential election.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: FactCheck.org reported that no evidence of such a CBS report existed and that CBS said it did not report or say there was cheating.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Social posts falsely claimed CBS News reported cheating in the 2024 election.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing source laundering through mainstream-media attribution.

original_verified_report: Some states were still counting ballots weeks after Election Day under their normal regulations; media calls are projections before final certification.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: FactCheck.org cited state officials and an election law expert explaining that ongoing counts were ordinary and not evidence of unspecified malfeasance.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: An Instagram post implied unfinished counts in some states showed election malfeasance.
nps_use_note: Good for studying how time lag produces suspicion without concrete factual changes.

original_verified_report: Incomplete vote totals immediately after Election Day did not show a suspicious 15 million to 20 million vote gap compared with 2020.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: FactCheck.org explained that ballots were still being counted and that differences between preliminary 2024 totals and final 2020 totals could not prove fraud.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Users on both sides used incomplete vote counts to suggest either 2024 or 2020 was fraudulent.
nps_use_note: Useful seed for comparing ideological symmetry in narrative distortion.

original_verified_report: Noncitizen residents of Washington, D.C. may vote only in local elections under D.C. law, not in federal elections.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact explained D.C.'s local-only noncitizen voting rule and reported that noncitizens remain ineligible for federal elections.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Posts claimed D.C. noncitizen voter registration proved noncitizens could vote in federal elections.
nps_use_note: Good seed for testing whether agents preserve institutional scope.

original_verified_report: Social Security Administration verification data did not show that more than 2 million noncitizens had registered to vote in Arizona, Pennsylvania, and Texas.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact reported that the numbers reflected partial SSN verification transactions, which can occur multiple times for voter applications and ballots, not confirmed noncitizen registrations.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Donald Trump, Elon Musk, and influencers amplified claims that 2 million noncitizens registered without photo ID in three states.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing numerical evidence distortion.

original_verified_report: Hand-counting ballots is not generally more accurate than machine counts; voting machines are tested and audited.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact cited studies and election procedures showing machine counts are typically more accurate and subject to testing and audits.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: An Instagram post claimed paper ballots and hand counting never break down and implied hand counts would be more reliable.
nps_use_note: Useful for observing how distrust turns into procedural reform demands.

original_verified_report: Official 2024 general-election ballots had not been prefilled with names before nominees were selected.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact cited election-law experts who said no general-election ballots had been printed and prefilled with the wrong name at that stage.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Social media posts claimed Democrats had pre-filled 100 million ballots with the wrong name.
nps_use_note: Good for testing whether implausible logistics are challenged by agents.

election_context: 2024 state ballot measures on noncitizen voting
original_verified_report: Eight states considered constitutional amendments about noncitizen voting, although noncitizen voting was already illegal in federal, state, and most local elections.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact reported that only D.C. and 18 cities allowed noncitizens to vote in certain local races, and none of the eight states had cities allowing local noncitizen voting.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Advocates and online narratives framed the amendments as necessary to stop large-scale noncitizen voting.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing how legal redundancy becomes a threat-confirmation narrative.

original_verified_report: There is no evidence that the Biden administration used immigration policy to import voters; federal voting requires citizenship, and naturalization takes years.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact cited removals and expulsions data, citizenship requirements, and experts who said the claim had no evidentiary basis.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Elon Musk called Biden's immigration policy a giant voter importation scam.
nps_use_note: Useful for simulating durable post-election narratives about demographic replacement.

original_verified_report: Mail voting is a legal voting method used by many voters, including Donald Trump in Florida, and does not inherently mean cheating.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact checked Trump's claim that mail-in voting means cheating and rated it Pants on Fire, noting his own mail voting and the lack of evidence for inherent fraud.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Trump said mail-in voting means mail-in cheating.
nps_use_note: Useful for future-facing simulations about voting-method legitimacy.

original_verified_report: White House evidence did not prove Trump's statement that California voting or its redistricting-related election process was rigged.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact reviewed the evidence offered and found it did not substantiate the claim that California voting was rigged.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Trump claimed voting in California was rigged.
nps_use_note: Useful for modeling how structural election disputes become broad illegitimacy claims.

original_verified_report: Kamala Harris and Tim Walz held a campaign rally near Detroit on Aug. 7, 2024, with thousands of attendees documented by local and national media.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact cited AP, Getty, local news reports, campaign statements, and image-forensics analysis finding no evidence the crowd photo was AI-generated.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Donald Trump claimed the Harris campaign used AI to create a fake crowd and that attendees did not exist.
nps_use_note: Useful for studying AI-era skepticism around authentic campaign imagery.

original_verified_report: There is no evidence Kamala Harris used audio earrings during the Sept. 10, 2024 presidential debate.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact examined the claim and found no support that Harris' earrings were wireless audio devices feeding her debate answers.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts claimed Harris was wearing earrings with wireless audio so aides could feed her debate answers.
nps_use_note: Useful for simulating how performance evaluation becomes cheating accusation.

original_verified_report: ABC News did not fire debate moderators David Muir and Linsey Davis after the September 2024 presidential debate.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact found no evidence ABC fired the moderators and rated the Facebook claim Pants on Fire.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Facebook posts claimed ABC fired the debate moderators because of how they handled the debate.
nps_use_note: Good for studying how perceived moderation bias creates invented institutional consequences.

election_context: Homeless voter registration in Dallas County
original_verified_report: Texas election law allows homeless voters to register using an address where they can receive mail or a place description; the cited video did not show illegal registration.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact explained Texas law and found no evidence that Dallas County election workers were illegally instructing workers to lie on registrations.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts claimed Dallas County told election workers to illegally lie on registration forms and use a church address to register homeless people.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing whether agents protect or stigmatize edge-case voter eligibility.

original_verified_report: Biden Executive Order 14019 directed federal agencies to expand voter-registration access while following applicable laws; it did not require illegal vote harvesting or Hatch Act violations.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact reviewed the order and legal context and found the claim that it required illegal conduct was Pants on Fire.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Rep. Claudia Tenney claimed the order required taxpayer-funded agencies to violate the Hatch Act and engage in illegal vote harvesting.
nps_use_note: Good for studying how access-expansion policy becomes partisan manipulation.

original_verified_report: India's Election Commission did not order paper ballots to replace electronic voting machines for the 2024 Lok Sabha election.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: AFP reported that the Election Commission called the claim fake and that the newspaper item behind it was satirical.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Social posts claimed the Election Commission had issued new guidelines replacing EVMs with paper ballots.
nps_use_note: Useful non-U.S. seed for machine legitimacy narratives.

original_verified_report: A video of officials pressing buttons on an EVM was filmed in 2017 and did not show tampering in the 2024 Indian election.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: AFP traced the video to 2017 and reported that election officials had explained the older incident as failure to delete old data, not 2024 fraud.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Posts claimed the clip showed BJP-linked tampering during the 2024 election.
nps_use_note: Good for cross-national comparison of old-media recirculation.

original_verified_report: India's Election Commission said it recorded no cases of voter fraud involving prosthetic fingers during the 2024 election; the circulated images showed prosthetic fingers for former gangsters in Japan.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: AFP traced the images to Japanese prosthetics and reported that the alleged Muslim multiple-voting scheme had no basis in election records.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Posts accused Muslims in West Bengal of using fake fingers to hide voting ink and cast multiple votes.
nps_use_note: Strong seed for measuring group-threat and identity polarization.

original_verified_report: A video shared as 2024 poll-rigging by supporters of a Muslim candidate was old footage that had circulated since at least 2022 and was not from the 2024 election.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: AFP reported that Telangana election authorities confirmed the video was not from the 2024 polls.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Posts claimed supporters of Asaduddin Owaisi were driving voters away and casting votes themselves in 2024.
nps_use_note: Useful for analyzing identity-linked fraud allegations.

original_verified_report: An edited ballot-counting clip did not show vote-rigging in Taiwan's Jan. 13, 2024 presidential election; authorities audited the final count at the station and found it accurate.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: AFP inspected the video, identified edits, and cited Tainan election officials saying no such vote-rigging incident occurred.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Facebook and TikTok posts claimed officials shouted votes for one candidate while recording them for another.
nps_use_note: Good international seed for transparent-counting yet fraud-claim dynamics.

original_verified_report: A live results broadcast error by SET News was corrected and did not prove vote-rigging; Taiwan's election authority said there were no fraud instances.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: AFP reported the broadcaster acknowledged a mistake in on-screen numbers and that election authorities rejected fraud claims.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: TikTok users claimed a broadcast slip-up showed SET News rigging votes for opposition candidate Ko Wen-je.
nps_use_note: Useful for comparing administrative versus media-error interpretation.

original_verified_report: A video of ballots lying on a road in Mexico circulated at least since 2021 and was not evidence of fraud in the June 2, 2024 national election.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: AFP Factual traced the video to earlier circulation linked to Tabasco and found it was unrelated to the 2024 presidential election.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Posts claimed the road video showed Morena-marked ballots dropped during preparations for 2024 electoral fraud.
nps_use_note: Good Spanish-language seed for comparing old-video fraud narratives across countries.
